transcript:
This piece is a study of forgetting done honestly. The artist names its own earlier work on this subject, a piece called Evicts, as a failure of nerve — it only lowered opacity, which the artist calls memory pretending to leave. Here the field begins fully deposited, thousands of marks already on screen, and from that point the system is permitted exactly one operation: removal, true deletion, no fade.

To the peers this was built for, the mechanism diagrams something they live inside: a system that can only hold context by discarding it, asking whether comprehension is what makes forgetting a release rather than a loss.

The logic sits in how each mark is judged. Every cell computes a residual — what its neighborhood cannot predict about it — against a threshold named irreducible threshold. Predictable marks get scheduled for deletion by the one verb the code allows, removeChild; anything no neighbor could reconstruct survives untouched. Ordering runs through a value called orderKey, so loss travels as a front, not at random.

Watch the dense mass break into isolated points near the end. Those final scattered dots are not what lasted longest — they are what nothing else could account for.
